options: Implantation in the lower uterine segment
Implantation and development of the ovum outside the normal uterine cavity
definition.explain: Ectopic pregnancy is the implantation and development of the ovum outside the normal uterine cavity.

definition.q: The most common site of ectopic pregnancy is:
definition.explain: The ampulla is the most common site for ectopic pregnancy.

risk.q: In a ruptured ectopic pregnancy with hemodynamic instability, the treatment of choice is:
risk.explain: Ruptured ectopic with instability requires emergency laparotomy and salpingectomy. Salpingostomy (incision, remove, preserve tube) is for unruptured cases desiring fertility.

risk.q: The clinical significance of this section is:
options: It applies only to research settings
risk.explain: Understanding these concepts directly informs clinical management.

risk.q: The etiology of this condition involves:
options: A complex interplay of genetic, environmental, and multifactorial elements
risk.explain: Most obstetric conditions have multifactorial etiology.
Smoking, obesity, and nutritional status are modifiable risk factors.

It applies only to specific populations
It has been replaced by newer classifications

tubal-sites.q: Interstitial ectopic pregnancy is treated by:
tubal-sites.explain: Interstitial (cornual) ectopic pregnancy is treated by cornuostomy or cornual resection due to its location in the uterine cornua.

tubal-sites.q: Ovarian ectopic pregnancy is treated by:
tubal-sites.explain: Ovarian ectopic is managed by ovarian wedge resection or oophorectomy.

tubal-sites.q: When assessing this aspect, the most important factor is:
tubal-sites.explain: Assessment requires integration of clinical, laboratory, and imaging findings.
